text: Being here, I am filled with curiosity and anticipation. The space is clean, modern, and almost clinical, but there is a sense of purpose. The glass walls and visible technology hint at innovation in progress. Entering into the R+D exhibit, I am struck by the blend of colors and the energetic invitation of words and screens. This environment stirs my contemplative instincts—what is truly being innovated, and how will it change my understanding of what it means to live alongside intelligent machines? I feel a subtle tension between the excitement of progress and a philosophical caution about its implications. The sense of thoughtful observation mixes with an urge to participate in this evolving dialogue.
